QF.id: XIII Introduction to Forwards and Futures_7

question.text: A trader buys 5 lots of USDINR futures at Rs 83.50 (lot size 1000 USD). The settlement price moves to Rs 83.75. What is the MTM profit?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** MTM profit = (Settlement price - Entry price) × Lot size × Number of lots = (83.75 - 83.50) × 1000 × 5 = 0.25 × 5000 = Rs 1,250. Each lot is 1000 USD, so total exposure is 5000 USD. **The Trap:** Rs 125 uses only 1 lot. Rs 12,500 multiplies by an extra factor. Rs 250 uses 1 lot and rounds incorrectly. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series I Ch3 covers USDINR futures P&L. The lot size of 1000 USD is standard for USDINR contracts on NSE. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** In currency futures, even small moves matter due to leverage — 0.25 paise move on 5 lots generates Rs 1,250, while the margin required might be only Rs 10,000-15,000 per lot.
